Advanced Energy Materials
Volume 16, Issue 13

1 April 2026

Cover Picture

Free Access
Interface Stabilization via In Situ Lithiated Sn Interlayer in All‐Solid‐State Li‐Metal Batteries: Toward Pellet‐Type Cell to Pouch‐Type Cell (Adv. Energy Mater. 13/2026)
Garam Kim, So-Jeong Im, Ji-Hyun Yu, Chul-Ho Lee, Yeonguk Son, Hyungyeon Cha, Seung Ho Choi, Ji-Sang Yu, Jaeik Hyun, Sunhye Yang, Heetaek Park, Jeong-Hee Choi, Youngoh Kim, Yoon-Cheol Ha, Ki-Hun Nam

e70762 | First Published: 03 April 2026

All-Solid-State Batteries

In article number e05910, Youngoh Kim, Yoon-Cheol Ha, Ki-Hun Nam, and co-workers illustrate a nano Sn interlayer at the Li metal/solid electrolyte interface, enabling regulated interfacial reactions and uniform Li-ion transport. By suppressing interfacial degradation and supporting stable plating/stripping, this strategy offers a scalable pathway toward durable all-solid-state Li-metal batteries, extending from laboratory cells to practical pouch-cell configurations.

    Back Cover

    Free Access
    Quantifying Lattice–Crack–Electrochemical Transport Coupling for Durable, Fast‐Charging Battery Cathodes (Adv. Energy Mater. 13/2026)
    Chen-Hao Tu, Chi-En Tseng, Wei-Cheng Lai, Chao-Hsiang Hsu, Jing-Sen Yang, Yu-Cheng Zheng, Huy Hoang Dang, Ping-Chun Tsai

    e70807 | First Published: 03 April 2026

    Fast-Charging Battery Cathodes

    While cracking in battery cathodes has been associated with improved rate performance, its quantitative impact on ion transport remains unclear. In article number e06427, Ping-Chun Tsai and co-workers establish a multiscale experimental and modelling framework that decouples fracture effects from intrinsic lattice diffusion, identifying fracture-generated interfaces as central contributors to enhanced ion transport and fast-charging behaviour.

      Issue Information

      Free Access
      Issue Information

      e70138 | First Published: 03 April 2026

        REVIEW

        Rational Design of Cellulose‐Based Gel Electrolytes for Next‐Generation Zinc‐Ion Batteries: Mechanisms, Advances, and Perspectives
        Jianchao Gao, Sisi Zhang, Chuang Jiang, Fangming Bai, Jiawei Yan, Wei Liu, Chenxiao Lin, Mingkai Liu

        e06462 | First Published: 20 January 2026

        This review summarizes recent advances in cellulose-based gel electrolytes for zinc-ion batteries, emphasizing their structural design, preparation strategies, and electrochemical mechanisms that enable dendrite suppression, interfacial stabilization, and the development of flexible, durable, and eco-friendly zinc-ion batteries for sustainable energy storage.

          Open Access
          Stage‐Specific Roles of Deep Eutectic Solvents in Recycling of Spent Lithium‐Ion Batteries
          Jingxiu Wang, Yan Wang, Yanqiu Lyu, Jianfeng Mao, Zaiping Guo

          e06741 | First Published: 21 January 2026

          Deep eutectic solvents (DESs) offer tunable acidity, redox, and coordination properties for selective recycling of spent lithium-ion battery cathodes. Through co-dissolution, single- and two-metal separations, DESs enable sustainable recovery of critical metals for closed-loop regeneration of battery-grade materials, advancing a circular economy for energy storage.

            Unleashing Sodium–Sulfur Battery Performance With Atomically Dispersed Single Atom Catalysts
            Sandip Maiti, Matthew T. Curnan, Noor Almusleh, Silpa Subhalaxmi, Dongwoo Shin, Ramanuj Narayan, Kakali Maiti, Jaehyun Hur

            e05686 | First Published: 28 January 2026

            We investigate how morphological features and well-characterized atomic structures are linked to catalytic performance enhancement in room temperature Na–S batteries, describing how single atom catalysts impact redox kinetics and reactive efficiency toward developing battery technologies.

              High‐Performance Triboelectric Nanogenerators for Marine Science: From Material Design, Intelligent Structures to System Integration
              Liqiang Liu, Jingxiang Xu, Wending Zuo, Jinda Lin, Walid A. Daoud, Tao Jiang, Zhong Lin Wang, Xiya Yang

              e06057 | First Published: 29 January 2026

              This review summarizes the high-output marine TENGs, focusing on material engineering, structural optimization, charge regulation and power management, hybridization strategies, and existing challenges in deployment in marine environments, serving as a valuable guide for researchers and engineers seeking to develop sustainable energy harvesting technologies for marine environments.

                RESEARCH ARTICLE

                Open Access
                Interface Stabilization via In Situ Lithiated Sn Interlayer in All‐Solid‐State Li‐Metal Batteries: Toward Pellet‐Type Cell to Pouch‐Type Cell
                Garam Kim, So-Jeong Im, Ji-Hyun Yu, Chul-Ho Lee, Yeonguk Son, Hyungyeon Cha, Seung Ho Choi, Ji-Sang Yu, Jaeik Hyun, Sunhye Yang, Heetaek Park, Jeong-Hee Choi, Youngoh Kim, Yoon-Cheol Ha, Ki-Hun Nam

                e05910 | First Published: 08 January 2026

                An in situ lithiated Sn interlayer forms a stable, lithiophilic, and conductive interface that enables durable and scalable pouch-type all-solid-state lithium metal batteries under low pressure.

                  Quantifying Lattice–Crack–Electrochemical Transport Coupling for Durable, Fast‐Charging Battery Cathodes
                  Chen-Hao Tu, Chi-En Tseng, Wei-Cheng Lai, Chao-Hsiang Hsu, Jing-Sen Yang, Yu-Cheng Zheng, Huy Hoang Dang, Ping-Chun Tsai

                  e06427 | First Published: 29 January 2026

                  This study establishes a quantitative lattice–crack–transport framework that mechanistically links microstructural fracture, Li+ bulk diffusion, and interfacial charge transfer in layered cathodes. It reveals that crack-generated surfaces dominate apparent transport enhancement in polycrystalline materials, while downsized single-crystal particles can achieve comparable fast-charging performance with superior durability—providing quantitative design rules for next-generation high-rate cathodes.

                    Decoupling Electrolyte Degradation Pathways With Diverse Li Plating Processes on Graphite Electrodes
                    Ke Zhang, Yonggang Hu, Shijun Tang, Wenxuan Hu, Jianrong Lin, Huiyan Zhang, Yufan Peng, Yuanzhe Tao, Yiqing Liao, Ying Lin, Lixuan Pan, Meifang Ding, Jinding Liang, Yimin Wei, Lufeng Yang, Jie Chen, Zhengliang Gong, Yanting Jin, Yong Yang

                    e05230 | First Published: 20 January 2026

                    Controlled overcharge in N/P < 1 cells is combined with practical fast-charging in N/P >1 cells to decouple lithium plating-induced side reactions from baseline graphite aging. Quantitative MST, NMR, and GC-MS analysis resolves inactive-lithium species and electrolyte component loss, revealing distinct electrolyte degradation pathways and guiding safer, longer-lasting fast-charge battery designs.

                      Holistic Anode Interface Engineering for High‐Efficiency and Stable Carbon‐Perovskite Solar Modules
                      Haodong Wu, Jungjin Yoon, Kavyashree S. Keremane, Luyao Zheng, Yuchen Hou, Shweta Sharma, Dong Yang, Sixing Xiong, Yanhui Zhang, Jin Qian, Shashank Priya, Bed Poudel, Kai Wang

                      e06097 | First Published: 22 January 2026

                      A holistic anode interface design is introduced to overcome interfacial contact and charge-transport limitations in planar carbon-based perovskite solar modules. Picosecond laser surface texturing and liquid-metal-modified carbon electrodes synergistically enhance interfacial conformity, carrier extraction, and moisture resistance, enabling highly efficient, stable large-area modules and advancing scalable, low-cost perovskite photovoltaic technologies.

                        Open Access
                        Leveraging Digital Advanced Manufacturing to Enable Polymer Electrolyte Fuel Cells With Ultrahigh Gravimetric Power Density
                        Hadi Heidary, Vahid Nasrollahi, Daniel Wilmot, Jacob Bracegirdle-Morais, Sophie C. Cox, Stefan Dimov, Moataz M. Attallah, Ahmad El-kharouf, Sara Walker, Robert Steinberger-Wilckens

                        e04454 | First Published: 21 January 2026

                        This study employs digital advanced manufacturing to develop lightweight, compact porous distributors as alternatives to conventional bipolar plates in PEM fuel cells. A graphene-coated nickel foam achieves a power density of 1.52 W cm−2, while titanium-based designs deliver lightweight solutions: an LPBF-fabricated Gyroid lattice reaches 1.36 W cm−2, and a laser-patterned non-homogeneous architecture achieves 1.62 W cm−2, corresponding to >10 kW L−1 and 9 kW kg−1.

                          Self‐Assembled Molecular Capsule via Metastable Soft Interface for Stable Aqueous Batteries
                          Kaiqiang Zhang, Haoning Xi, Shengtao Yang, Qinhan Yang, Jilei Ye, Yuping Wu

                          e70668 | First Published: 21 January 2026

                          This study reports a self-assembled molecular capsule formed by amphiphilic polymers (Pluronic F127) on organic redox particles to stabilize both oxidized and reduced species in aqueous battery systems. The resulting soft, metastable interface survives dry-wet transitions upon contact with the aqueous electrolyte. It regulates water and ion access, suppresses electrode degradation, and markedly improves cycling stability.

                            Mitigating Hydrogen Evolution Reaction with Polyzwitterionic Hydrogel Electrolyte in Aqueous Aluminum‐ion Batteries
                            Jin-Jie Liew, Bei-Er Jia, Dan-Yang Wang, Ziyue Wen, Hong-Han Choo, Jinxuan Song, Qiang Zhu, Man-Fai Ng, Qingyu Yan

                            e70677 | First Published: 21 January 2026

                            The polyzwitterionic hydrogel electrolyte (PZHE) operates under lean-water conditions with abundant bound water molecules; this effectively suppresses free water activity and mitigates parasitic reactions associated with the Al anode. Zwitterionic groups construct ionic migration channels across the polymeric matrix, ensuring high ionic conductivity and uniform ion flux, thereby facilitating reversible Al plating/stripping, enhancing overall electrochemical performance.

                              A Mineral‐Biopolymer Synergistic Quasi‐Solid‐State Electrolyte for Long‐Lasting Zinc Metal Batteries
                              Chi Chen, Fulong Li, Yating Gao, Gai Li, Lutong Shan, Yicai Pan, Yongqiang Yang, Wen Chen, Xiaoxiao Liang, Jing Li, Zhenyue Xing, Peng Rao, Zhenye Kang, Yingjie Hua, Xiaodong Shi, Xinlong Tian

                              e05241 | First Published: 23 January 2026

                              DLM@SA@Zn composite electrolyte delivers fast zinc diffusion kinetics and strong interaction with polyiodides, which physically separates the redox reactions of metallic zinc to Zn2+ ions (Zn anode side) and iodine to iodides (AC@I2 cathode side), effectively endows the uniform zinc deposition, and eliminates the active iodine dissolution and polyiodide shuttle during the cycling process.

                                Dampening Lithium Charge Density by Clustering Solvents and Anions to Tame Lithium De‐Coordination Energy for Low‐Temperature Lithium‐Metal Batteries
                                Jin-Hao Zhang, Yu Zhang, Zhi-Yuan Gu, Jin-Xiu Chen, Chen-Hao Yu, Ayaulym Belgibayeva, Gulnur Kalimuldina, Xin-Bing Cheng, Long Kong

                                e05551 | First Published: 26 January 2026

                                Both the bulky electrolyte and the interfacial layer gain benefits from such cluster solvates. In the bulky electrolyte, it has been observed that the electron-donating nature of LiNO3 reduces the positive charge of Li+, thereby dampening the interaction between Li+ and TEP ligands (Ede2<Ede1). In the interfacial layer, cluster solvates facilitate the interfacial charge exchange to hasten Li transport kinetics (N2>N1).

                                  Open Access
                                  Thin Reinforced Sulfonated Poly(phenylene sulfone) Membranes for Durable Water Electrolysis: Suppressed Swelling and Enhanced Stability Over 2000 Hours
                                  Clara Schare, Giorgi Titvinidze, Christian Piesold, Edgar Cruz Ortiz, Nodar Dumbadze, Michael Schuster, Klaus-Dieter Kreuer, Carolin Klose, Andreas Münchinger

                                  e05888 | First Published: 20 January 2026

                                  Hydrocarbon membranes have the potential to significantly enhance the efficiency of water electrolyzers. However, their application is often limited by insufficient stability resulting from excessive swelling. This work presents a strategy to achieve efficient, safe and stable operation of a 24 µm thin hydrocarbon membrane by combining a modified polymer structure with a reinforcement layer.

                                    In Situ TEM Reveals the Hard Carbon Sodium Storage Mechanism and Discovers the Sodium Carbide
                                    Huimin Cao, Yizhao Zhao, Zhiyi Huang, Xingxiang Ji, Shoujuan Wang, Wanqi Duan, Yuebin Xi, Yuanwei Sun, Fangong Kong, Yu Liu, Huan Wang

                                    e05191 | First Published: 23 January 2026

                                    Based on lignin thermal stability, can regulate hard carbon microstructures, topological cavity-rich hard carbon for enhancing sodium storage performance. The sodium storage process of hard carbon cavity-rich structure and the formation of new sodium carbide structure were verified by in situ TEM and GITT.

                                      Manipulating Cu Surface Carbophobicity with Pb for Efficient CO‐to‐Ethanol Electroconversion
                                      Yingjie He, Yao Tan, Qiuwen Liu, Bojing Sun, Qin Chen, Baixiong Liu, Kang Liu, Min Liu

                                      e06794 | First Published: 23 January 2026

                                      Enhanced carbophobicity of Cu catalyst was obtained via doping impressive electronegativity Pb element, which steered the hydrogenation site at β-C of *CH2CHO intermediates, preserving the C─O bond and achieving excellent ethanol formation.

                                        Heterocycle‐Bridged Short Sulfur‐Chain Polymers for Polysulfide‐Suppressing Cathodes in Lithium‐Organosulfide Batteries
                                        Xingkai Ma, Huan Li, Junchuan Liang, Yaoda Wang, Tianyu Shen, Xinmei Song, Zuoao Wu, Huaizhu Wang, Lina Qin, Tianchen Yu, Zuoxiu Tie, Zhong Jin

                                        e06388 | First Published: 23 January 2026

                                        A heterocycle-bridged short sulfur-chain polymer (PPZTS) is rationally designed as a cathode material for Li–S batteries, effectively suppressing polysulfide shuttling and promoting Li+ diffusion, thereby enabling high capacity, excellent rate performance, and long-term cycling stability.

                                          Open Access
                                          Enhanced Fast‐Charging Performance of High‐Mass‐Loading Mn‐Rich Li[Mn1‐xFex]PO4 Cathodes via LiF‐Less Cathode–Electrolyte Interphase
                                          Bonyoung Ku, Lahyeon Jang, Hyunji Kweon, Jinho Ahn, Sunha Hwang, Jihoe Lee, Myungeun Choi, Sunyoung Yoo, Kwangho Yoo, Kyu-Young Park, Jongsoon Kim

                                          e06130 | First Published: 24 January 2026

                                          LiF-rich@LMFP develops a CEI enriched with electronically and ionically insulating LiF that impedes interfacial charge transfer during fast charging. By suppressing interfacial LiF formation, LiF-less@LMFP accelerates Li+ exchange and lowers overpotentials, delivering 1.6-fold higher capacity at 5C and ∼87% retention after 100 cycles under practical high-mass-loading conditions, with consistently reliable performance under varied current rates.

                                            Double‐Coordination Additives Induced High‐Entropy SEI for Stable Anode‐Less Sodium Metal Batteries
                                            Zhengda Lv, Xindan Li, Wenbin Li, Jun Luo, Yunhua Ling, Yue Sun, Xiujuan Lu, Shuai Guo, Shaohua Ge, Enhui Wang, Mingrui Yang, Weihua Chen

                                            e06756 | First Published: 24 January 2026

                                            A double-coordination electrolyte strategy is developed to engineer a high-entropy, inorganic-rich SEI. This ultrathin (3.7 nm) mechanically robust shield lowers nucleation barriers, enabling uniform sodium deposition with a remarkable average Coulombic efficiency of 99.58%. Consequently, it realizes anode-less batteries delivering a high energy density of 327.54 Wh kg−1 with superior stability.

                                              Wheel‐Hub‐Inspired Silicon Anodes with Superior Stress Tolerance for High‐Energy Lithium‐Ion Batteries
                                              Xiangze Xin, Qianhui Yin, Ziyun Zhao, Jiangshan Qi, Tianze Xu, Zhenshen Li, Fangbing Li, Fanqi Chen, Shichao Wu, Quan-Hong Yang

                                              e06650 | First Published: 27 January 2026

                                              The wheel-hub-inspired silicon-carbon architecture (WH-Si@C) is realized via enclosed near surface mesopores, simultaneously overcoming internal lithiation expansion stress and external calendering pressure. This stress-tolerant anodes enables crushing strength (372.62 MPa) and a stable interface, achieving outstanding electrochemical performance and offering a practical way to build stable Si anodes for next generation energy storage applications.

                                                Multi‐Domain Energy Harvesting with Mode‐Dependent Magneto‐Mechano‐Electric Coupling
                                                Liwei Dong, Chaoyang Zhao, Shuai Qu, Xin Li, Jiashun Li, Guobiao Hu, Chengjia Han, Yu Zhang, Ping Wang, Fan Yang, Zhong Lin Wang, Yaowen Yang

                                                e06114 | First Published: 26 January 2026

                                                This work presents a dual-mode magneto-mechano-electric harvester with a mode decoupling design for simultaneous harvesting of distinct-frequency magnetic field and vibration energy. Dual magnetic coupling and internal resonance significantly enhance performance. An optimized SECE circuit enables efficient extraction of weak electrical energy and ultra-weak-field wireless sensor operation, demonstrating strong potential for real-world rail-side applications.

                                                  Open Access
                                                  Selective Ion‐Blocking Strategy for Stabilizing Perovskite Tandem Solar Cells
                                                  Sunwoo Kim, Hangyeol Choi, Doyun Im, Yeonghun Yun, Won Chang Choi, Jungchul Yun, Hyun Seo Park, Nagyeong Hyeon, Dong Hyun Kim, Gill Sang Han, Junesic Park, Gwang Min Sun, Jun Hong Noh, Ji-Sang Park, Yohan Yoon, Sangwook Lee

                                                  e06634 | First Published: 28 January 2026

                                                  A selective ion-blocking Li─T interlayer is introduced at LiF-passivated perovskite interfaces to suppress fluorine diffusion while preserving the beneficial interfacial passivation of LiF. This ion-blocking strategy markedly improves the long-term operational stability of both single-junction and monolithic all-perovskite tandem cells under continuous illumination, without any penalty in photovoltaic performance.

                                                    A Stable and Reliable Li–In Alloy Counter Electrode for Sulfide‐Based All‐Solid‐State Batteries
                                                    Gengzhong Lin, Yicheng Deng, Guo Tang, Hui Li, Yuliang Cao, Yongjin Fang, Xinping Ai

                                                    e06291 | First Published: 28 January 2026

                                                    A homogeneous LiIn/In biphasic alloy counter electrode is successfully fabricated through a chemical pre-lithiation strategy. The as-prepared electrode exhibits superior reaction kinetics and high lithium-extraction capacity, overcoming the limitations of conventional cold-pressed counter electrodes and enabling reliable assessment of initially lithium-free electrodes for sulfide-based all-solid-state batteries.

                                                      How Electrolytes Control the Activity and Stability of Fe‐N‐C for Oxygen Reduction Reaction in Acidic Media
                                                      Shaojie Jing, Zhouhao Zhu, Wencai Liu, Chaogang Ban, Yucui Xiang, Jing Fan, Zhiping Zeng, Jiangping Ma, Li-Yong Gan, Xiaoyuan Zhou

                                                      e05944 | First Published: 28 January 2026

                                                      Fe-N-C single-atom catalysts exhibit electrolyte-dependent behavior in acidic ORR. Using grand canonical simulations and experiments, we reveal that electrolyte anions dictate axial coordination: SO42− forms stable, active SO4-Fe* sites, while H2O-Fe* in HClO4 shows lower activity and stability, highlighting electrolyte identity as the key performance determinant.

                                                        Hydroxide/Selenide Heterostructures With Built‐In Electric Fields Enabling Reconstruction for Advanced Quasi‐Solid‐State Supercapacitors
                                                        Shaoxiong Du, Wang Yang, Yao Yao, Chen Zhang, Ruoyao Feng, Wenjie Zhu, Zhenfei Gao, Yongfeng Li

                                                        e06211 | First Published: 28 January 2026

                                                        This work reveals the role of hydroxide/selenide heterostructures for efficient electrochemical activation and optimizing charge transfer kinetics. A detailed analysis is conducted on the mechanisms of BIEF generation, charge redistribution, and accelerated phase transformation. The reconstructed a-NiCoSe@Ni(OH)2 with abundant oxygen vacancies achieves exceptional areal capacitance in hybrid supercapacitors.
